title: toxicodendron radicans subsp. pubens
intro: Toxicodendron radicans subsp. pubens, also known as the eastern poison ivy, is a subspecies of the poison ivy plant that is native to eastern North America. The plant contains urushiol, a toxic oil that can cause an allergic reaction and skin irritation in some individuals. However, the plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and fever. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies like tinctures, salves, and infusions, and has been used to promote health benefits such as skin rejuvenation, pain relief, and immune system support.
